notes: Replaced SWE-rebench stub test.sh (FAIL_TO_PASS empty -> fallback to go test ./... overall pass rate, ceiling >=0.99 across all v0.4.3 trials) with 6 weighted F2P gates (sum=1.00) targeting the actual fix: G1 TestUpdateCommand passes with >=4 subtests (table-driven), G2 package-level var <name> = os.Executable indirection, G3 updateCommand body no longer calls os.Executable() directly, G4 path-specificity (>=3 of /Cellar/, /opt/homebrew/, /linuxbrew/, /mise/installs/ AND loose '/homebrew/' + '/mise/' literals removed), G5 TestUpdateCommand assigns to the override variable (name extracted from src, not hard-coded), G6 t.Run(...) with >=3 named cases. All gates are implementation-agnostic: variable name is discovered from the source file rather than pinned to 'executablePath' (avoids the SWE-bench-Verified narrowness trap flagged in prior curator log). Validation locally on darwin/arm64 with go1.26.3: baseline (HEAD=1278f43b at parent of canonical patch) -> reward 0.000000 (all 6 gates fail); after `git apply canonical.patch` -> reward 1.000000 (all 6 gates pass). P2P_REGRESSION informational only. Weighted-replace tail: existing=0, inner=0, p2p_failed=False, f2p_any_pass guard intact. Manifest test_manifest.yaml updated to match.
